subject: Add Common Helm Chart "mariadb-galera"
commit_message:
Add Common Helm Chart "mariadb-galera"

A new common helm chart that will deploy a Galera cluster for MariaDB.
Please note that this chart is still work in progress and more features
will be added or improved.

Change-Id: Ia4487666798f83d2869c35bcfaacc5516068f194
Issue-ID: OOM-758
Signed-off-by: vitalied <vitalied@amdocs.com>

Patch Set 4: Verified-1

==> Linting mariadb-galera
[INFO] Chart.yaml: icon is recommended
[ERROR] templates/: render error in "mariadb-galera/templates/statefulset.yaml": template: mariadb-galera/templates/statefulset.yaml:26:20: executing "mariadb-galera/templates/statefulset.yaml" at <.Values.init.enabled>: can't evaluate field enabled in type interface {}

This is still present in the statefulset:
{{- if .Values.init.enabled }}
        - name: mariadb-docker-entrypoint

I think if you clean this up, along with the left over volumes and 
re-test by making, and deploying we can finally merge this.
Patch Set 5: Published edit on patch set 4.

Patch Set 5:

Vitalie, how did you deploy/test this chart?

I am starting with an empty k8s cluster and I am adding it in the onap requirements.yaml and deploying it as part of the umbrella.

It does not work however as it fails to pull the image.  Looks like the image secret registry reference needs to be put in the statefulset.


  Warning  Failed                 6m (x3 over 13m)   kubelet, k8s-5     Failed to pull image "nexus3.onap.org:10001/adfinissygroup/k8s-mariadb-galera-centos:v002": rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Tag v002 not found in repository nexus3.onap.org:10001/adfinissygroup/k8s-mariadb-galera-centos

That image does exist and I can manually pull it using docker pull but it needs to work as part of a helm chart.

Mandeep, the pull image secret needs to be attached to service account. This should be done automatically after the namespace creation.

 > >   Failed to pull image "nexus3.onap.org:10001/adfinissygroup/k8s-mariadb-galera-centos:v002":
 > > rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Tag v002 not found in repository
 > > nexus3.onap.org:10001/adfinissygroup/k8s-mariadb-galera-centos
 > >
 > > That image does exist and I can manually pull it using docker
 > pull
 > > but it needs to work as part of a helm chart.
 > 
 > Mandeep, the pull image secret needs to be attached to service
 > account. This should be done automatically after the namespace
 > creation.

You asked to remove the chart that creates the pull secret, I hope you understand that is absolutely wrong to keep a reference to a pull secret in the statefulset that was not created in the context of the same chart, as it brakes the integrity of the chart.

If we want to control at the common project level as common artifacts then service account, common pull secret, other security policies needs to be created after namespace provisioning stage or before running any other charts.

Let me know what approach you want to follow and I will change it:
1.	Secret Chart and  Secret reference in Statefulset.
2.	Assign the secret at the service account level (this is proper approach)

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+2 Verified+1

With the addition of the common shared secret back into the statefulset it comes up now.

NAME                       READY     STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
dev-mariadb-galera-0       1/1       Running   0          15m
dev-mariadb-galera-1       1/1       Running   0          14m
dev-mariadb-galera-2       1/1       Running   0          13m

I agree that the service account approach sounds like the right thing to do but since every other chart is referencing the pull secret from the onap umbrella chart changing the model is a future task to solve.

Note: to be able to deploy this as part of onap you must apply the following changes:
diff --git a/kubernetes/onap/requirements.yaml b/kubernetes/onap/requirements.yaml
index 8d4f42c..2c19c3d 100644
--- a/kubernetes/onap/requirements.yaml
+++ b/kubernetes/onap/requirements.yaml
@@ -112,3 +112,7 @@ dependencies:
     version: ~2.0.0
     repository: '@local'
     condition: vnfsdk.enabled
+  - name: mariadb-galera
+    version: ~2.0.0
+    repository: '@local'
+    condition: mariadb-galera.enabled
\ No newline at end of file

and then turn it on:
mariadb-galera:
  enabled: true
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda

Patch Set 6:

guys investigating cpu limits for LOG-376 - I don't think the cpu limit of 2 here is actually kicking in - rendered pod is empty  "resources": {},

Patch Set 6:

> guys investigating cpu limits for LOG-376 - I don't think the cpu
 > limit of 2 here is actually kicking in - rendered pod is empty 
 > "resources": {},

Hi Michael,

The resources in the statefulset.yaml will be empty only when the values.yaml that is provided as argument or project specific values.yaml for mariadb-galera chart will have empty entry for resources: 
resources: {}

I have the logs of the helm install or dry run that shows that resources are being populated fine.

# Source: mariadb-galera/templates/statefulset.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1beta1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: mariadb-galera-my-release
  namespace: onap
  labels:
    app: mariadb_galera
    chart: mariadb_galera_0.0.1
    release: my_release
    heritage: tiller
spec:
  serviceName: mariadb-galera-my-release
  replicas: 3
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mariadb_galera
        release: my_release
    spec:
      volumes:
        - name: mariadb-docker-entrypoint
          emptyDir: {}
        - name: mariadb-conf
          configMap:
            name: mariadb-galera-my-release-confd-configmap
        - name: localtime
          hostPath:
            path: /etc/localtime
      imagePullSecrets:
      - name: mariadb-galera-my-release-docker-registry-key
      containers:
        - name: mariadb-galera-init
          image: nexus3.onap.org:10001/axeclbr/git
          env:
            - name: GIT_REPO
              value: https://github.com/vitaliedicusara/docker-entrypoint-initdb
          command: ["sh", "-c", "git clone $(GIT_REPO) . && watch -n 600 git pull"]
          workingDir: /var/lib/data
          volumeMounts:
            - name: mariadb-docker-entrypoint
              mountPath: /var/lib/data
        - name: mariadb-galera
          image: nexus3.onap.org:10001/adfinissygroup/k8s-mariadb-galera-centos:v004
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
          - containerPort: 3306
            name: mariadb
          - containerPort: 4444
            name: sst
          - containerPort: 4567
            name: replication
          - containerPort: 4568
            name: ist
          # disable liveness probe when breakpoints set in debugger
          # so K8s doesn't restart unresponsive container
          readinessProbe:
          #   tcpSocket:
          #     port: 3306
            exec:
              command:
              - /usr/share/container-scripts/mysql/readiness-probe.sh
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
          env:
            - name: POD_NAMESPACE
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  apiVersion: v1
                  fieldPath: metadata.namespace
            - name: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mariadb-galera-my-release
                  key: db-root-password
            - name: MYSQL_USER
              value: ""
            - name: MYSQL_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mariadb-galera-my-release
                  key: user-password
            - name: MYSQL_DATABASE
              value: ""
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: 2
              memory: 4Gi
            requests:
              cpu: 2
              memory: 4Gi
          volumeMounts:
          - mountPath: /etc/localtime
            name: localtime
            readOnly: true
          - mountPath: /etc/mysql/conf.d
            name: mariadb-conf
          - mountPath: /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
            name: mariadb-docker-entrypoint

 > guys investigating cpu limits for LOG-376 - I don't think the cpu
 > limit of 2 here is actually kicking in - rendered pod is empty 
 > "resources": {},
